persons.arguments_against: Strongest case it is wrong: 4 Cottle officials exist and 'Government Printer'/'Assistant Government Printer' could in principle be held by successive same-name men. Why it fails: the bio carries two initials (H. C.) that agree, and the stint reproduces the bio's exact three-step progression - 'Second-Assistant Government Printer' (1888), 'Assistant Government Printer' (1890s-1906), 'Government Printer' (1907 on) - within the same Civil Establishment; age 31 in 1888 fits a man born 1857, and 14 edition-years co-occur. This precise grade-by-grade career is uniquely identifying.

evidence_cited.fact: 2nd astl. gov't. printer, Ceylon, Nov., 1885; astl. gov't. printer, 1888

persons.would_overturn: A second H. C. Cottle in the printing department, or a date conflict in the 2nd-asst -> asst -> govt printer progression.

notes: Two initials, exact grade-by-grade printer progression, and 14 edition cooccurrences make this certain.
